recommendations.description: Merge 6 duplicate rules checking same fields
Fix rules checking wrong fields (deletion protection, public access, encryption overlaps)
recommendations.impact: HIGH - Ensures rules validate correct compliance requirements

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are elasticache service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_cache_clusters, describe_replication_groups) belong to elasticache service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use elasticache API methods

yaml_structure.field_paths: All field paths match emit structure

deletion_protection_rules.issue: ElastiCache API does not expose a direct 'DeletionProtection' field in describe_cache_clusters or describe_replication_groups responses
deletion_protection_rules.current_check: cluster: item.MemberClusters exists, node: item.CacheClusterId exists
deletion_protection_rules.recommended_check: N/A - ElastiCache deletion protection is managed via IAM policies/tags, not API fields
deletion_protection_rules.impact: MEDIUM - Rules cannot directly verify deletion protection via API
deletion_protection_rules.fix_complexity: Requires alternative approach (IAM policy checks, tag checks, or accepting limitation)
deletion_protection_rules.note: ElastiCache deletion protection is typically enforced through IAM policies preventing DeleteCacheCluster/DeleteReplicationGroup actions, not through a resource attribute

public_access_rules.issue: ElastiCache API does not expose a direct 'PubliclyAccessible' field
public_access_rules.recommended_check: CacheSubnetGroupName existence is a reasonable proxy - clusters with subnet groups are in VPCs (not publicly accessible)
public_access_rules.impact: LOW - Current check is acceptable proxy (subnet group = VPC = not publicly accessible)
public_access_rules.fix_complexity: Current implementation is reasonable given API limitations
public_access_rules.note: ElastiCache clusters with CacheSubnetGroupName are in VPCs and not publicly accessible. Clusters without subnet groups are in EC2-Classic (deprecated).

encryption_overlap.issue: Multiple rules check the same field (AtRestEncryptionEnabled) but with different intents
encryption_overlap.recommended_check: encryption_at_rest_cmek_configured correctly checks both AtRestEncryptionEnabled and KmsKeyId
encryption_overlap.impact: LOW - Rules are functionally correct but semantically overlap
encryption_overlap.fix_complexity: Consolidation opportunity - rules can be merged per metadata_review_report consolidation suggestions
encryption_overlap.note: These rules check the same field but represent different compliance requirements. Consolidation is recommended.

notes.validation_approach: Validated YAML structure, field paths, discovery chains, and operators. All structural elements are correct.
notes.functional_validation: Tested against 5 AWS accounts - 0 execution errors. YAML structure is correct.
notes.api_limitations: ElastiCache API has limitations for certain security checks (deletion protection, public access) that require workarounds or alternative approaches.
notes.overall_assessment: YAML implementation is structurally correct and executes without errors. Field paths match emit structure. Discovery chains are properly configured. Some rules have limitations due to ElastiCache API constraints, which are documented for future enhancement.
